game_info.title: Ticket to Ride - Railway Adventure
game_info.concept: **Les Aventuriers du Rail®:** Ticket to Ride is a turn-based digital board game where 2-5 players compete to build railway routes across a map, connecting cities to complete destination tickets and earn points. Players begin with 45 train cars, 4 train cards, and 3 destination tickets (keeping at least 2), then take one of three actions per turn: drawing two train cards (from five face-up cards or blind from the deck), drawing three new destination tickets (keeping at least one), or claiming a route by playing a set of matching colored train cards equal to the route's length. Claimed routes are visually represented by placing the player's train cars on the digital board, earning immediate points based on the route's length, while destination tickets award bonus points if completed or penalize if uncompleted at game end. The game concludes when any player has two or fewer train cars remaining, triggering a final round for all players, after which bonus points are awarded for the longest continuous railway and the player with the highest total score wins. Controls involve tapping to draw cards or tickets, and dragging-and-dropping train cards onto desired routes for claiming, with AI opponents offering 3-5 difficulty levels and online multiplayer supporting both real-time and asynchronous play.
game_info.description: Build railway routes across a simplified map to connect cities and complete destination tickets. Collect matching colored train cards to claim routes between cities. Each completed destination ticket earns you points, while uncompleted ones cost you points. The game ends when you run low on train cars, and the player with the highest score wins!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate menu selections and map
Space: Confirm selection / Draw cards / Claim route
Shift: Toggle between drawing cards and claiming routes
Z: View your destination tickets
Enter: Start game
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart to main menu

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic game mechanics including drawing train cards, viewing the map, and navigating the UI. This validates that the core interaction systems work correctly and the player can perform fundamental actions without errors.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Draw train cards repeatedly to build up a hand, then attempt to navigate the map and view destination tickets. Cycle through different actions to ensure all basic controls respond properly.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The game should allow smooth transitions between drawing cards and viewing tickets. Train card collection should update the player's hand correctly. No crashes or state inconsistencies should occur during basic interactions.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests the win condition by implementing an optimal strategy to complete destination tickets and maximize score. This validates that routes can be claimed, tickets can be completed, and the game properly transitions to the win state.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Focus on completing short, high-value routes that connect destination ticket cities. Collect matching colored cards efficiently, claim routes strategically to complete tickets, and maintain enough train cars to finish the game with a winning score.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The automated player should successfully claim multiple routes, complete at least one destination ticket, and achieve a positive final score. The game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN when train cars run low, displaying the final score and victory message.

TEST_3.test_description: Tests route claiming mechanics by attempting to claim various routes of different lengths and colors. Validates that the card payment system works correctly and prevents invalid claims.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Collect cards of specific colors, then attempt to claim routes matching those colors. Try to claim routes with insufficient cards to test validation, then claim valid routes to test success cases.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Routes should only be claimed when the player has enough matching cards. Train cars should decrease appropriately, and claimed routes should be marked visually. Invalid claims should be rejected without changing game state.
